titleStmt.title: Letter from Olive Duffin to her mother, November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Olive Duffin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Olive to her mother, telling her about Celia, her sister who was suspected of having mumps and is in quarantine. She then informs her mother that it is only a flu and that she will be back on duty in a few days. Celia's address is 5 Eastfield Gardens, Landsworth, whcih is not an Irish address

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
D2109/9/4/B 2 Tablot Square Wed:Nov 1915 Dearest Mamma Just a line to tell you that Celia
has influenza. She worried she could not
smell me yesterday. This morning a note
came saying she had mumps, &
was summoned to quarantine. I have just been
telephoning the hospital & a nice person
came and said it was the “flue” and she was getting
on well & would be back on duty in a
few days! I’ve not gone to see her because
of this reason. I thought I ought not to
chance catching it off her. I have just
written to Celia, telling her to let me know
if she wants anything. And I will send her
some flowers and fruit. Her address is
5 Eastfield Gardens, Landsworth. l am so relieved it is not mumps,
just off to see Iris & then off to
Eastmine. Your loving,
Olive
